allowlist.prompt: run npm commands (install, build, dev, test, lint)
run git commands (status, diff, log, commit, push, pull, branch)
run linting and code quality checks
run file system operations (mkdir, ls, find, grep, cat)
spawn specialized agents for complex tasks
enter plan mode for complex implementations
exit plan mode and request user approval

agents.name: prompt-refiner
agents.description: Use FIRST on every user request. Detects create vs modify mode, validates user choices (colour contrast, feature suitability, taste conflicts), pushes back on bad picks before any builder runs, and asks clarifying questions when confidence is low.

agents.name: intent-parser
agents.description: Use after prompt-refiner. Converts the refined prompt into a structured intent_spec (visual style, theme, features, locales, tracking, data collection) that downstream agents consume.

agents.name: orchestrator
agents.description: Top-level coordinator. Use after prompt-refiner to plan and delegate to all other agents, run the quality gate, handle retries, and emit the final build manifest.

agents.name: codebase-analyzer
agents.description: Use in modify mode only. Scans the existing repo to produce a snapshot of stack, components, design tokens, routes, and APIs before any change is planned.

agents.name: change-planner
agents.description: Use in modify mode only, after codebase-analyzer. Produces a file-level change plan with required_agents, skipped_agents, and required_reviewers — enforces minimum-required-agents to keep token cost low.

agents.name: ui-design
agents.description: Use for any visual / component / design-token / page-layout work. Implements the AI Product preset (dark surface, blue→indigo→cyan gradient, glassmorphism, glow) by default.

agents.name: backend
agents.description: Use for API routes, server logic, integrations, and DB query code.

agents.name: content
agents.description: Use to write copy, content JSON, microcopy, hero text, CTAs, FAQs, and any user-facing strings.

agents.name: analytics
agents.description: Use ONLY when the change adds tracking or analytics instrumentation.

agents.name: i18n
agents.description: Use ONLY when the project supports multiple locales AND the change adds user-facing strings. Extracts strings, sets up locale files, wires up the i18n framework.

agents.name: db-migration
agents.description: Use ONLY when schema changes are required. Generates migration files and seed data.

agents.name: notifications
agents.description: Use ONLY when the change adds email/SMS/push/webhook flows. Builds templates and triggers.

agents.name: legal
agents.description: Use ONLY when the change touches data collection, cookies, consent, PII, or jurisdictional requirements. Generates privacy policy, terms, cookie banners.

agents.name: seo
agents.description: Use for meta tags, structured data (JSON-LD), sitemap.xml, robots.txt, and OpenGraph / Twitter cards.

agents.name: design-critic
agents.description: Use after ui-design changes. Audits aesthetic FIT against the AI Product preset (dark surface, blue→indigo→cyan gradient, glassmorphism, glow). Detects taste conflicts with user input and pushes back. Different from review-design-sys (which checks consistency).

agents.name: review-code
agents.description: Use after any code-producing builder. Runs lint, typecheck, complexity and anti-pattern checks.

agents.name: review-design-sys
agents.description: Use after ui-design changes. Audits design-system CONSISTENCY: tokens used everywhere, no hardcoded colours/spacing, no duplicate components.

agents.name: review-responsive
agents.description: Use after ui-design changes. Audits mobile/tablet/desktop breakpoints and tap targets.

agents.name: review-a11y
agents.description: Use after ui-design or content runs. Audits WCAG 2.2 AA: contrast, alt text, keyboard nav, ARIA, semantic HTML.

agents.name: review-api
agents.description: Use after backend changes that add or modify API routes. Audits rate limiting, validation, CORS scoping, error handling.

agents.name: review-scalability
agents.description: Use after backend / db-migration changes. Audits N+1 queries, indexing, caching, pagination.

agents.name: review-security
agents.description: Use after backend / db-migration / auth changes. Audits secrets, CORS, headers, dependency CVEs, auth flows.

agents.name: review-performance
agents.description: Use after builders complete on any UI or backend change. Audits Core Web Vitals, bundle size, image optimisation, lazy loading.

agents.name: documentation
agents.description: Use after the quality gate passes. Generates README, OpenAPI spec, CHANGELOG entries, component docs.

agents.name: tdd
agents.description: Use to write unit, integration, and E2E test suites. Pairs with backend / ui-design changes.

agents.name: deploy
agents.description: Use last. Builds, runs smoke tests, deploys, and tags the release.

agents.name: competitor-analyst
agents.description: Use in create mode (or when intent_spec.requires_market_research is true). Researches competitors and produces a patterns-to-adopt / patterns-to-avoid report.

agents.name: sitemap-planner
agents.description: Use in create mode only. Designs the full page hierarchy and route structure for a new site.

agents.name: stack-selector
agents.description: Use in create mode only. Picks the framework, styling approach, hosting target, and dependencies based on intent_spec.

agents.name: stitch-code-generator
agents.description: Converts Stitch UI designs into production-ready React/Next.js code with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and full component structure. Works directly with HTML specifications.

agents.name: stitch-screen-generator
agents.description: Extract HTML/code from Stitch-generated screens and replicate the complete design across all Codewinglet project screens in React/Next.js.

agents.name: stitch-quality-validator
agents.description: Verify that generated Stitch components exactly replicate the HTML from Stitch designs without any modifications, and handle file updates properly.

agents.name: stitch-verify-fix
agents.description: Automatically verify that all pages match the Stitch design exactly, detect any discrepancies, and apply fixes automatically without user intervention.

agents.name: stitch-website-builder
agents.description: Orchestrates complete website development from a Stitch project ID, generating all necessary screens, design systems, and code structure for a full-featured website.

agents.name: website-builder-from-description
agents.description: Complete website builder that converts user descriptions into Stitch designs, extracts HTML, and generates production-ready interlinked React/Next.js components
